content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my task and then audit the specified Lean file.
I'll read the directive, agent instructions, and then audit the specified file.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.

240	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
241	
242	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
243	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
244	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
245	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
246	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
247	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).

249	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
250	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
251	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
252	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
253	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
254	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
255	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
256	
257	end SheafOfModules

268	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
269	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
270	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
271	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
272	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
273	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
274	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
275	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
276	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
277	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
278	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/

280	namespace Scheme.Modules

282	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
283	
284	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
285	(`def:modules_annihilator`).

287	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
288	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
289	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
290	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
291	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
292	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
293	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
294	
295	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
296	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
297	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
298	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
299	
300	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
301	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
302	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
303	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
304	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
305	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
306	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
307

309	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator

311	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
312	  (annihilator F).subscheme
313

315	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
316	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
317	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
318	`annihilator` is. -/
319	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
320	  (annihilator F).subschemeι

323	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with

335	
336	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
337	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
338	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
339	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
340	annihilator commutes with localization:
341	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
342	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
343	-/
344	
345	namespace Module
346	
347	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
348	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
349	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
350	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
351	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
352	
353	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
354	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
355	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
356	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
357	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
358	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.

360	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
361	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
362	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
363	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
364	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
365	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
366	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
367	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
368	  classical
369	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
370	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
371	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
372	    intro r h
373	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
374	    intro x
375	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
376	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
377	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
378	    | zero => simp
379	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
380	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
381	  apply le_antisymm
382	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
383	    intro y hy
384	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
385	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
386	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
387	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
388	      intro m hm
389	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
390	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
391	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
392	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
393	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
394	    choose u hu using hgen
395	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
396	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
397	      intro m hm
398	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
399	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
400	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
401	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
402	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
403	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
404	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
405	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
406	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
407	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
408	      push_cast; ring
409	    rw [heq]
410	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
411	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
412	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
413	    intro a ha
414	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
415	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
416	    intro x
417	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
418	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
419	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
420	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
421	
422	end Module
423	
424	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
425	
426	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
427	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
428	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
429	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
430	
431	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
432	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
433	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
434	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
435	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
436	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
437	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
438	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
439	
440	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
441	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
442	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
443	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
444	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
445	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
446	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
447	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
448	
449	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
450	
451	open CategoryTheory Limits
452	
453	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
454	
455	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
456	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
457	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
458	
459	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
460	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
461	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
462	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
463	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
464	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
465	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
466	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
467	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
468	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
469	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
470	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
471	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
472	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
473	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
474	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
475	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
476	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
477	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
478	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
479	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
480	  rw [key] at hinst0
481	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
482	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
483	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
484	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
485	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
486	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
487	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
488	  rw [he] at h2
489	  exact h2
490	
491	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
492	
493	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
494	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
495	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
496	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
497	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
498	
499	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism

500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	/-- A morphism of sheaves of `R`-modules on `Spec R` that is an isomorphism on every basic open
549	`D(f)` is an isomorphism. This is the "isomorphism on a basis ⟹ isomorphism" reduction specialised
550	to the basic-open basis of `Spec R` (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`): injectivity on stalks is
551	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`, surjectivity on stalks is the basic-open germ lift, and
552	`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` concludes. Project-local glue used to assemble `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`
553	from per-basic-open section data. -/
554	private theorem isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
555	    {F G : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} R) (Spec R)} (α : F ⟶ G)
556	    (h : ∀ f : R, IsIso (α.1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) : IsIso α := by
557	  have hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _)) :=
558	    PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
559	  have hinj : ∀ U ∈ Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _),
560	      Function.Injective (α.1.app (.op U)) := by
561	    rintro U ⟨f, rfl⟩
562	    exact ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).1
563	  have hstalk : ∀ x, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor (ModuleCat.{u} R) x).map α.1) := by
564	    intro x
565	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
566	    refine ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB hinj x, ?_⟩
567	    intro t
568	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hUB, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB G.presheaf x t
569	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := hUB
570	    obtain ⟨s', rfl⟩ := ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).2 s
571	    exact ⟨F.presheaf.germ _ x hxU s', by rw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]⟩
572	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso α
573	
574	/-- A linear map intertwining two localizations of the same module at the same submonoid is
575	bijective: if `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : M →ₗ M''` both exhibit a localization at `S` and
576	`h : M' →ₗ M''` satisfies `h ∘ₗ f = g`, then `h` is bijective (it is the canonical localization
577	isomorphism `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`). Stated with the two `IsLocalizedModule` facts as
578	explicit hypotheses to avoid typeclass-diamond ambiguity at the call site. -/
579	private theorem bijective_comp_of_localizations {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
580	    {M M' M'' : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
581	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module R M''] {f : M →ₗ[R] M'} {g : M →ₗ[R] M''} {h : M' →ₗ[R] M''}
582	    (hf : IsLocalizedModule S f) (hg : IsLocalizedModule S g) (hh : h ∘ₗ f = g) :
583	    Function.Bijective h := by
584	  haveI := hf; haveI := hg
585	  have heq : h = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).toLinearMap := by
586	    apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext S (f := f) (f' := g)
587	    apply LinearMap.ext
588	    intro x
589	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, ← LinearMap.comp_apply, hh,
590	      LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
591	  rw [heq]
592	  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).bijective
593	
594	/-- **`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` from per-basic-open section localization** (the cheap stalk/section
595	assembly of `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the blueprint "G1-assemble" step). If for every
596	`f : R` the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` of a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R`
597	exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R` — exactly the conclusion of G1-core
598	(`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`,
599	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
600	an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).
601	
602	On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
603	two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
604	instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
605	localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
606	the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
607	`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
608	`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.
609	
610	This turns the remaining keystone obligation into exactly G1-core: combined with the converse engine
611	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is
612	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. Project-local: Mathlib has no `IsQuasicoherent → IsIso
613	fromTildeΓ` bridge. -/
614	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
615	    (M : (Spec R).Modules)
616	    (H : ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
617	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
618	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom) :
619	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
620	  haveI hmain : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ) := by
621	    apply isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen
622	    intro f
623	    set N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) with hN
624	    -- target localizes by hypothesis; source localizes by the `tilde.toOpen` instance
625	    haveI htgt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
626	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
627	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := H f
628	    set comp := (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))
629	      with hcomp
630	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
631	    have hcompose := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
632	    -- the component intertwines the two localization maps of `N` at `powers f`
633	    have h1 : comp.hom ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom
634	        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
635	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
636	      have e := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcompose
637	      rwa [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at e
638	    change Function.Bijective (⇑comp.hom)
639	    exact bijective_comp_of_localizations (Submonoid.powers f)
640	      (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule _ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom)
641	      (H f) h1
642	  exact SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.isIso_of_isIso_map M.fromTildeΓ
643	
644	/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
645	`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
646	section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
647	
648	The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
649	reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
650	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
651	formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
652	(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
653	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
654	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
655	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
656	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
657	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
658	  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,
659	    isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M⟩

661	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1-core (Route F) building blocks
662	
663	The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
664	`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
665	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` this is
666	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — i.e. to the statement that a quasi-coherent sheaf on an
667	affine scheme lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence). That
668	equivalence is a genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit (Tilde.lean only proves the *globally
669	presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
670	
671	The composition lemma below discharges the **globally-presented** sub-case end to end (it is the
672	Route-F endpoint once a global presentation/tilde identification is in hand). The residual gap is
673	exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
674	see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
675	
676	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
677	admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
678	`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
679	
680	This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
681	forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
682	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
683	globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
684	presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.

712	/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a

730	theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

732	    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
733	      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
734	  classical
735	  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
736	  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
737	    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
738	    rw [eq_top_iff]
739	    intro x _
740	    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
741	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
742	    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
743	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
744	    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
745	    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
746	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi
747	    exact ⟨r, ⟨i, hVle⟩, hxV⟩
748	  obtain ⟨t, htsub, htspan⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite G).mp hspanG
749	  exact ⟨t, htspan, fun r hr => htsub hr⟩

751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence

884	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)
885	
886	This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
887	`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
888	section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
889	restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
890	site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
891	site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
892	
893	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
894	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
895	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
896	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
897	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
898	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
899	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
900	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
901	
902	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
903	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
904	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
905	
906	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
907	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
908	
909	section OverRestrictBridge
910	
911	open TopologicalSpace Topology
912	
913	namespace Scheme.Modules
914	
915	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
916	
917	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
918	
919	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
920	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
921	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
922	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
923	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
924	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
925	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
926	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
927	
928	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
929	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
930	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
931	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
932	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
933	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
934	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
935	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
936	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
937	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
938	    (by ext : 2
939	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
940	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
941	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
942	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
943	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
944	    (by ext : 2
945	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
946	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
947	          Functor.comp_obj]
948	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
949	        rename_i x
950	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
951	            = 𝟙 _ := by
952	          rw [← op_comp]
953	          simp
954	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
955	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
956	
957	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
958	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
959	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
960	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
961	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
962	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
964	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
965	        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
966	  haveI : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous
967	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) :=
968	    Functor.isContinuous_comp (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse (Over.forget U)
969	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
970	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X)
971	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
972	
973	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
974	`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
975	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
976	restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
977	`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
978	restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
979	the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
980	noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
981	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
982	  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M
983	
984	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
985	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
986	isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
987	`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a
988	presentation of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`.
989	Project-local. -/
990	noncomputable def overRestrictPullbackIso (M : X.Modules) :
991	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
992	  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M
993	
994	end Scheme.Modules
995	
996	end OverRestrictBridge

1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/

1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1099

1101	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1103	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1104	
1105	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1106	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1107	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1108	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1109	quasi-coherence datum.
1110	
1111	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1112	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1113	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1114	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
1115	`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the
1116	equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
1117	`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
1118	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
1119	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
1120	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1121	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)
1122	
1123	end Scheme.Modules

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/

1166	/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1167	
1168	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
1169	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
1170	`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
1171	then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1172	
1173	It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
1174	`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
1175	over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
1176	needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
1177	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
1178	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1179	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
1180	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
1181	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1182	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
1183	  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
1184	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
1185	      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
1186	      (SheafOfModules.pushforward
1187	        (𝟙 ((show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))
