1189	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
1203	  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _

1209	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
1210	`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` fact needed by
1211	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Supplied via `haveI` at use sites (instance resolution cannot invert
1212	`φ.inv.base`). Project-local. -/
1213	theorem opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1214	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
1215	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
1216	  infer_instance

1218	/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).
1219	
1220	For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
1221	structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
1222	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
1223	of schemes is `Final` (`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso`). This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum
1224	consumed by `Presentation.map` along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
1225	Project-local. -/
1226	noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1227	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1228	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by
1229	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := opensMap_final_of_schemeIso φ
1230	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.pushforward (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance
1231	  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))

1233	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1234	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1235	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1236	/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).
1237	
1238	Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
1239	`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
1240	`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
1241	`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:

1245	    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
1246	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
1247	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1} (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv) :=
1248	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.inv).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
1249	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
1250	    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ)

1258	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
1259	`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
1260	the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is

1274	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=

1284	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
1285	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).
1286	
1287	Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with

1299	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

1302	    IsIso (@Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ

1315	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D: the section-localization descent
1316	
1317	The keystone `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` reduces (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks
1318	`lemma-invert-f-sections`) to a finite-cover sheaf-gluing argument.  The single geometric input
1319	is the **per-cover-element** fact that on each `D(r)` of a finite cover `{D(r_j)}` of `Spec R`
1320	(with `D(r_j) ≤ q.X i`), the basic-open restriction `Γ(M, D(r)) → Γ(M, D(f) ⊓ D(r))` is a
1321	localization at `powers f` — this is exactly the P1 local-tilde data transported to sections, and
1322	is the gated hypothesis `Hfr` below.  Given `Hfr` (for every `r` whose `D(r)` sits inside a cover
1323	member, hence also for the overlaps `D(r r')`), the descent is pure sheaf theory: separatedness
1324	gives the `exists_of_eq` field, gluing the patched compatible family gives `surj'`, and the global
1325	`map_units` field holds for arbitrary `M` (`map_units_restrict_basicOpen`). -/
1326	
1327	/-- A finite family `t` spanning `R` gives a basic-open cover of `Spec R`: the supremum of the
1328	`D(r)` over `r ∈ t` is `⊤`.  Project-local glue feeding the sheaf-gluing reduction of the
1329	section-localization descent. -/

1689	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `IsLocalizedModule` transport for gap1-D Hfr
1690	
1691	The section-transport iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` is only an additive-group (`Ab`) isomorphism,
1692	semilinear over the *source-scheme* section ring, whereas the `Hfr` hypothesis of
1693	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover` is an `IsLocalizedModule` statement `R`-linear over
1694	the base ring `R`. Two Mathlib-absent transport ingredients bridge the gap:
1695	
1696	* **(I)** `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` — transport `IsLocalizedModule S g` across a
1697	  ring-iso-`σ`-semilinear `AddEquiv` pair. Mathlib only has the same-ring `of_linearEquiv` /
1698	  `of_linearEquiv_right`; the section iso crosses a ring iso, so this is the genuine gap.
1699	* **(II)** `isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap` — a localization at
1700	  `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring `Rr` (here `R` localized at `r`) is, after
1701	  restriction of scalars, a localization at `powers f` over `R`.

1703	Both are pure module algebra and Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
1704	
1705	/-- **(I) Ring-iso-semilinear `IsLocalizedModule` transport.** Given a ring isomorphism
1706	`σ : R ≃+* R'`, two `σ`-semilinear additive isomorphisms `e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁`, `e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂` (i.e.
1707	`eᵢ (a • x) = σ a • eᵢ x`), and an `R'`-linear map `h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂` intertwining a localization
1708	map `g` with the `eᵢ` (`h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)`), the map `h` is a localization at the image submonoid
1709	`S.map σ`. Mathlib only provides the same-ring `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`; this crosses a
1710	ring iso, the form needed to turn the `Ab`/semilinear section iso `gammaPullbackImageIso` into the
1711	`R`-linear `Hfr` data. Project-local. -/
1712	theorem isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear
1713	    {R R' : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] (σ : R ≃+* R')
1714	    {M₁ M₂ N₁ N₂ : Type*}
1715	    [AddCommGroup M₁] [Module R M₁] [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂]
1716	    [AddCommGroup N₁] [Module R' N₁] [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module R' N₂]
1717	    (S : Submonoid R)
1718	    (g : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂) [IsLocalizedModule S g]
1719	    (e₁ : M₁ ≃+ N₁) (e₂ : M₂ ≃+ N₂)
1720	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)
1721	    (he₂ : ∀ (a : R) (x : M₂), e₂ (a • x) = σ a • e₂ x)
1722	    (h : N₁ →ₗ[R'] N₂)
1723	    (hh : ∀ x, h (e₁ x) = e₂ (g x)) :
1724	    IsLocalizedModule (S.map (σ : R →+* R')) h where
1725	  map_units x := by
1726	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hsx⟩ := x.2
1727	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1728	    have hsrc := IsLocalizedModule.map_units g ⟨s, hs⟩
1729	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hsrc
1730	    have hfun : (⇑(algebraMap R' (Module.End R' N₂) (↑x : R')))
1731	        = ⇑e₂ ∘ ⇑(algebraMap R (Module.End R M₂) (⟨s, hs⟩ : S)) ∘ ⇑e₂.symm := by
1732	      funext y
1733	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply,
1734	        Module.algebraMap_end_apply, he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply]
1735	      congr 1
1736	      exact hsx.symm
1737	    rw [hfun]
1738	    exact e₂.bijective.comp (hsrc.comp e₂.symm.bijective)
1739	  surj y := by
1740	    obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S g (e₂.symm y)
1741	    refine ⟨⟨e₁ x, ⟨σ ↑s, ↑s, s.2, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1742	    have he : e₂ ((↑s : R) • e₂.symm y) = e₂ (g x) := congrArg e₂ hx
1743	    rw [he₂, e₂.apply_symm_apply, ← hh] at he
1744	    exact he
1745	  exists_of_eq {y₁ y₂} heq := by
1746	    have h1 : e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₁)) = e₂ (g (e₁.symm y₂)) := by
1747	      rw [← hh, ← hh, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply]; exact heq
1748	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S) (f := g) (e₂.injective h1)
1749	    refine ⟨⟨σ ↑c, ↑c, c.2, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
1750	    have hc' : (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₁ = (↑c : R) • e₁.symm y₂ := hc
1751	    have hcc := congrArg e₁ hc'
1752	    rw [he₁, he₁, e₁.apply_symm_apply, e₁.apply_symm_apply] at hcc
1753	    exact hcc

1755	/-- **(II) Localization at a base-changed submonoid descends to the base ring.** If `g` is
1756	`Rr`-linear and exhibits a localization at `powers (algebraMap R Rr f)` over a base-changed ring
1757	`Rr` (an algebra over `R` — here `R` localized at some `r`), then its restriction of scalars to `R`
1758	is a localization
1759	at `powers f` over `R`. This lets the `R_r`-level localization that P1 (`IsIso fromTildeΓ`) produces

1765	    [AddCommGroup M₂] [Module R M₂] [Module Rr M₂] [IsScalarTower R Rr M₂]
1766	    (f : R) (g : M₁ →ₗ[Rr] M₂)

1784	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1785	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑s := hn
1786	    refine ⟨⟨x, ⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1787	    have hsmul : (f ^ n : R) • y = (↑s : Rr) • y := by
1788	      rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]

1790	    rw [hsmul, LinearMap.coe_restrictScalars]; exact hx
1791	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} heq := by
1792	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Submonoid.powers (algebraMap R Rr f))
1793	      (f := g) heq
1794	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
1795	    have hn' : (algebraMap R Rr f) ^ n = ↑c := hn
1796	    refine ⟨⟨f ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
1797	    have e1 : (f ^ n : R) • x₁ = (↑c : Rr) • x₁ := by rw [← hn', ← map_pow, algebraMap_smul]

1802	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — gap1-D Hfr: pullback-section transport
1803	
1804	The remaining gated ingredient of the named gap1 keystone
1805	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` is the **section-level** analogue of P1's object-level
1806	transport: for an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` and a sheaf of modules `M` on `Y`, the sections of the
1807	geometric pullback `(pullback f).obj M` over an open `U ⊆ X` are canonically identified with the
1808	sections of `M` over the image `f ''ᵁ U`.
1809	
1810	The construction is `Γ(-, U)` applied to the inverse of Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f`
1811	(`restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f`) at `M`, using the *definitional* identity
1812	`Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` (`Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`, `rfl`). Because
1813	both sides are `Γ(-, U)` of a single fixed module isomorphism, naturality in `U` (intertwining the
1814	presheaf restriction maps) is free: it is the naturality of the underlying abelian-presheaf
1815	morphism. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; project-bespoke. -/
1816	
1817	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the image**
1818	(gap1, Hfr section transport, general open). For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, a sheaf of modules
1819	`M` on `Y`, and an open `U ⊆ X`, the additive groups of sections satisfy
1820	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)`. This is `Γ(-, U)` of `(restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)⁻¹`
1821	at `M`; the codomain is `Γ((restrictFunctor f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` definitionally. Naturality
1822	in `U` is `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality`. -/
1823	noncomputable def gammaPullbackImageIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
1824	    (M : Y.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
1825	    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M, U) ≅ Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) :=
1826	  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op U)).mapIso
1827	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M)
1828	
1829	/-- **The pullback-section comparison intertwines the restriction maps** (gap1, Hfr, naturality).
1830	For opens `V ≤ U` of `X`, `gammaPullbackImageIso` commutes with the presheaf restriction maps of
1831	`(pullback f).obj M` and of `M` (along the image inclusion `f ''ᵁ V ≤ f ''ᵁ U`). This is the
1832	naturality of the underlying morphism of abelian presheaves. -/
1833	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_naturality {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
1834	    (M : Y.Modules) {U V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
1835	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op ≫ (gammaPullbackImageIso f M V).hom
1836	      = (gammaPullbackImageIso f M U).hom ≫ M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := by
1837	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).symm.app M).hom.mapPresheaf).naturality i.op
1838	
1839	/-- **Global sections of a pullback along an open immersion are sections over the range**
1840	(gap1, Hfr section transport). The `U = ⊤` instance of `gammaPullbackImageIso`:
1841	`Γ((pullback f).obj M, ⊤) ≅ Γ(M, f.opensRange)`, using `f ''ᵁ ⊤ = f.opensRange`. Once this lands the
1842	named-form descent `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent` and gap1 follow. -/
1843	noncomputable def gammaPullbackTopIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
1844	    (M : Y.Modules) :

1846	  gammaPullbackImageIso f M ⊤ ≪≫ eqToIso (by rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange])

1848	/-- **Open-immersion structure-sheaf ring iso on an image open** (gap1, Hfr semilinearity).
1849	For an open immersion `j : X ⟶ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the immersion is an isomorphism onto its

1852	`σ` along which `gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear` is semilinear, the form bridge (I)
1853	`isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear` consumes.
1854

1856	section ring acting on the pullback module's sections). Project-local. -/
1857	noncomputable def gammaImageRingEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
1858	    (V : X.Opens) : Γ(X, V) ≃+* Γ(Y, j ''ᵁ V) :=
1859	  (j.appIso V).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.symm

1863	section of the structure sheaf and `x` a section of the pullback module,
1864	`hom (a • x) = σ_V a • hom x`. The pullback-side action is the structure-sheaf action through
1865	the pullback's `mapPresheaf`; the action on the `M` side is `M`'s action through `σ_V`.

1867	theorem gammaPullbackImageIso_hom_semilinear {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (j : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion j]
1868	    (M : Y.Modules) (V : X.Opens) (a : Γ(X, V))
1869	    (x : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj M, V)) :
1870	    (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom (a • x)
1871	      = gammaImageRingEquiv j V a • (gammaPullbackImageIso j M V).hom x := by
1872	  -- `gammaPullbackImageIso j M V`'s forward map is `Γ(-, V)` of the `Ab`-morphism

1902	`powers (algebraMap R A f)` over `A`, then bridge (II)

1911	    [AddCommGroup N₂] [Module A N₂] [Module R N₂] [IsScalarTower R A N₂]

1914	    (he₁ : ∀ (a : S) (x : M₁), e₁ (a • x) = σ a • e₁ x)

Let me check if `hRES` and `hS1` are actually used in subsequent steps. Looking at the code:

- `haveI hRES := RESULT'` at line 2157 — this introduces `RESULT'` as a typeclass instance. `RESULT'` itself is an `IsLocalizedModule` fact. The `haveI` makes it available for `inferInstance`/typeclass search.
- `step1` uses `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right` which needs `hRES` as an instance.
- Similarly, `haveI hS1 := step1` registers `step1` as an instance for typeclass resolution, which `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` then relies on.

So both are being used implicitly through instance resolution rather than appearing explicitly in the code — `haveI` both binds the name and registers it in the local typeclass context for `inferInstance` to find.

No orphaned `have`/`letI` found. Now I'm examining the `letI` instance more carefully to understand how it's constructing the module homomorphism. ''ᵁ D(f))` from the natural module structure over `Γ(X, j ''ᵁ D(f))`, the `letI` doesn't conflict with any existing instance—it's legitimately introducing a new one via restriction of scalars. The `show … from restrictₗ M ii` pattern then packages this restricted linear map to establish that the localization satisfies the required `IsLocalizedModule` property. The `Over` category morphisms require a commutativity condition on the `w` field, but when both objects use `homOfLE`, this becomes a proposition in `Opens X` which is subsingletonized, so `Subsingleton.elim` correctly handles the proof obligation. I'm also analyzing how `eqToHom` is being used in the localization section around lines 2151-2156. The `eqToHom` of an equality between opens produces an isomorphism when applied through the presheaf, so wrapping it with `asIso` is justified. I'm checking whether the swap of opens in `Opposite.op` creates a non-defeq equality that could cause issues downstream. The `eqToHom` here is the right approach since `U` and `j ''ᵁ ⊤` are propositionally equal but not definitionally equal, and the downstream `Subsingleton.elim` correctly equates the presheaf map compositions using the uniqueness of morphisms between the same opens. Looking at the audit findings, there are no must-fix issues, but `gammaPullbackTopIso` is defined in the Lean code yet never actually used in any proof—it only appears in the blueprint, making it dead code that inflates the file.

Beyond that, there are stale iteration number comments (iter-176/iter-177) in four top-level docstrings that reference the pre-extraction project timeline and are misleading even though the bodies are `sorry`. The comment at line 2059 about the large multi-step assembly is sparse on profiling details despite the heartbeat budget being 1.6M, and there's a somewhat unclear variable name `hj` at line 2464.

# Lean Audit Report

## Slug
quot-iter043

## Iteration
043

## Scope
- files audited: 1
- files skipped (per directive): 0

---

## Per-file checklist

### AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

- **outdated comments**: 4 flagged (stale iter-176/177 references in 4 top-level docstrings)
- **suspect definitions**: 4 flagged (pre-existing `sorry` bodies on the 4 blueprint-pinned declarations)
- **dead-end proofs**: 0
- **bad practices**: 1 flagged (minor orphaned `set...with` name bindings)
- **excuse-comments**: 0
- **notes**:

  **Focus: `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1` (~line 2457)**

  - **Statement honesty**: CLEAN. Hypotheses `hU : IsAffineOpen U`, `hP1 : IsIso (fromTildeΓ ...)`, `f : Γ(X,U)` plus two typeclass parameters for `Module` and `IsScalarTower` are all genuine and non-vacuous. Conclusion `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (restrictBasicOpenₗ M f)` is the correct gap2 statement. No placeholder hypotheses, no vacuous form.

  - **Proof authenticity**: GENUINE transport. The proof correctly:
    1. Sets `j = hU.fromSpec`, computes `eT : j ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` and `eB : j ''ᵁ D(f) = X.basicOpen f`.
    2. Defines `f_im = σ f` (the section under the gammaImageRingEquiv), proves `hf' : rfl`.
    3. Calls `section_localization_hfr_aux_general M j hP1 f_im f hf'` for the core localization over `A = Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤)`.
    4. Builds ring iso `ρ : Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤) ≃+* Γ(X, U)` via `asIso (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom eT.symm).op)`.
    5. Proves `ρ f_im = f` using the crux `fromSpec_image_top_section_coherence`.
    6. Transports via bridge (I) `isLocalizedModule_of_ringEquiv_semilinear`.
    No defeq abuse, no circular reasoning.

  - **`letI : Module … := Module.compHom …` + `show … from restrictₗ …` idiom** (lines 2475–2481): LEGITIMATE instance plumbing. There is no natural `Module Γ(X, j ''ᵁ ⊤) Γ(M, j ''ᵁ D(f))` instance in scope — the natural module is over `Γ(X, j ''ᵁ D(f))`. The `letI` creates a new restriction-of-scalars instance along `ii.op`, required so that `restrictₗ M ii` has the type `section_localization_hfr_aux_general`'s conclusion expects. The `show … from` coercion forces Lean to pick this `letI`-backed instance. Does NOT weaken or duplicate any prior in-scope instance.

  - **Orphaned `have`/`letI` in `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_hP1`**: Three orphaned `with`-name bindings (minor noise; see minor issues below). All substantive `have`/`letI` bindings are consumed.

  **Focus: `change`/`eqToHom`/`Subsingleton.elim`**

  - **`Subsingleton.elim` uses** (lines 837, 2176, 2302, 2520, 2533): ALL legitimate. Every call resolves a morphism-equality goal in a thin category (`Opens X` or `Over (Opens X)` where morphisms between a fixed pair of opens are unique by the poset structure). No non-defeq goal is silently discharged.

  - **`eqToHom` uses** (lines 2151–2156, 2483–2498, 2300–2305): ALL transport along genuine propositional equalities of opens computed by named lemmas (`image_top_eq_opensRange`, `fromSpec_image_basicOpen`, etc.). The `asIso (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom …).op)` pattern correctly uses the fact that presheaf applied to `eqToHom` of an open equality is an isomorphism (its inverse is `eqToHom` of the symmetric equality). No silent non-defeq goal swap detected.

  - **`change` steps**: All `change` calls unfold definitionally equal terms (e.g. expanding `e₁ (a • x)` to the presheaf-map form, or rewriting a composition as a `≫`). No non-defeq swaps.

  **Focus: `maxHeartbeats` bumps**

  All 7 bump sites are justified:
